name: Buzzer
tagline: Generates audible beeps and tones — active buzzers buzz on their own, passive ones play melodies
description: A buzzer is an electromechanical or piezoelectric audio output device. Active buzzers have a built-in oscillator and buzz continuously when power is applied. Passive buzzers require an external PWM signal (via tone()) and can play melodies and custom frequencies. Both types are commonly used for alerts, alarms, feedback, and music in Arduino projects.

specs.passiveType: Frequency-driven — use tone(pin, frequency) to play sounds

pinout.name: Positive
pinout.desc: Connect to 5V (active) or to Arduino digital pin (passive)

wiringNote: Active buzzer: connect + to a digital output pin and − to GND. Use digitalWrite(pin, HIGH) to sound it. Passive buzzer: connect same way but drive with tone(pin, frequency, duration) for melody playback. Add a 100Ω resistor in series to protect the Arduino pin.
libraryInstall: No installation required. tone() is built into the Arduino core.
commonMistakes: Using tone() on an active buzzer → produces erratic sounds, not expected tones
Driving buzzer directly without a resistor → can exceed pin current limit (40mA max)
Confusing active and passive buzzers — active has a small circle on top, passive has no marking
Calling tone() on pin 11 while using Servo library → conflict, use a different pin
